entries.definition: An organization that sells goods to end customers through physical stores, supported by procurement, logistics, and financial operations.
entries.business_usage: Top-level entity for strategy, org design, and consolidated reporting.

entries.definition: A brick-and-mortar location where customers browse and purchase goods and where local inventory and staff are managed.
entries.business_usage: Store network planning, staffing, compliance, and location-level P&L.

entries.definition: A distinct site used to scope inventory, sales, and operational activity within the retail network.
entries.business_usage: Inventory allocation, replenishment triggers, and cross-location performance comparison.

entries.definition: The classification of a store by size, layout, or trade area focus, such as superstore, convenience, or neighbourhood format.
entries.business_usage: Network planning, range allocation, and labour modelling.
entries.notes: Inferred; common retail segmentation dimension.

entries.definition: The non-customer-facing storage area within a store where reserve inventory is held before replenishment to the shop floor.
entries.business_usage: In-store replenishment scheduling and shrink control.
entries.notes: Inferred from physical-store operating model.

entries.definition: A sellable item or SKU identified for catalog, pricing, and inventory purposes.
entries.business_usage: Merchandising, assortment planning, pricing, and inventory management.

entries.definition: A grouping of related products used to organise the range for merchandising, reporting, and buying.
entries.business_usage: Category management, planogram assignment, and buyer responsibility mapping.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard way retailers organise products.

entries.definition: The multi-level classification tree (e.g. division > department > category > sub-category > SKU) that structures the product range.
entries.business_usage: Drill-down reporting, assortment rules, and margin analysis at every level.
entries.notes: Inferred; underpins most retail analytics.

entries.definition: The manufacturer or label identity associated with a product, influencing customer perception and vendor negotiations.
entries.business_usage: Brand-level sales reporting, shelf-space negotiation, and marketing.
entries.notes: Inferred; relevant to product and supplier management.

entries.definition: The standard quantity designation for a product (e.g. each, pack, kilogram, litre) used in ordering, selling, and inventory.
entries.business_usage: Purchase-order quantities, POS scanning, and stock counting.
entries.notes: Inferred; essential for inventory and purchasing accuracy.

entries.definition: A machine-readable code (e.g. EAN, UPC) printed on a product or shelf label that uniquely identifies a SKU at point of sale and in logistics.
entries.business_usage: POS scanning, goods receipt, and stock-take accuracy.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard retail product identifier.

entries.definition: The set of products carried at a location or across the chain, shaped by category strategy and supplier availability.
entries.business_usage: Range reviews, planograms, and new-product introductions.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing products across locations.

entries.definition: A visual schematic prescribing the placement of products on shelves and fixtures within a store.
entries.business_usage: Space planning, visual merchandising compliance, and shelf-productivity analysis.
entries.notes: Inferred; common physical-store merchandising tool.

entries.definition: An external party that provides goods to the retailer, typically under negotiated commercial terms.
entries.business_usage: Procurement, vendor scorecards, cost negotiation, and accounts payable.

entries.definition: A contract or set of terms governing price, delivery lead time, volume commitments, and returns policy between the retailer and a supplier.
entries.business_usage: Procurement planning, cost benchmarking, and dispute resolution.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard accompaniment to purchasing from suppliers.

entries.definition: The smallest purchase volume a supplier will accept per order or per SKU.
entries.business_usage: Replenishment planning, cost modelling, and supplier negotiation.
entries.notes: Inferred; common purchasing constraint.

entries.definition: The elapsed time between placing a purchase order and receiving the goods at the designated location.
entries.business_usage: Reorder-point calculation, safety-stock setting, and delivery promise.
entries.notes: Inferred; critical to inventory planning.

entries.definition: The buyer of goods in a retail context, whether identified through a loyalty programme or anonymous at the point of sale.
entries.business_usage: Sales analysis, customer service, returns eligibility, and marketing segmentation.

entries.definition: A scheme that rewards repeat customers with points, discounts, or benefits in exchange for identified purchasing behaviour.
entries.business_usage: Customer retention, personalised marketing, and basket-level analytics.
entries.notes: Inferred; common retail customer engagement mechanism.

entries.definition: A grouping of customers by shared characteristics such as spending level, frequency, geography, or demographics.
entries.business_usage: Targeted promotions, range tailoring, and lifetime-value analysis.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard retail marketing practice.

entries.definition: A structured record of goods requested or committed, whether a customer sales order or a supplier purchase order.
entries.business_usage: Fulfilment tracking, customer service, and financial monitoring.

entries.definition: A customer-facing order recording one or more items requested for purchase, along with delivery and payment expectations.
entries.business_usage: Fulfilment, delivery scheduling, and revenue recognition.
entries.notes: Inferred as the customer-side specialisation of Order.

entries.definition: A single product entry on an order specifying the item, quantity, price, and any applicable discount.
entries.business_usage: Picking, invoicing, and line-level margin analysis.
entries.notes: Inferred; orders are composed of lines.

entries.definition: An order or order line that cannot be fulfilled immediately due to insufficient stock and is queued for future delivery.
entries.business_usage: Customer communication, replenishment priority, and lost-sale analysis.
entries.notes: Inferred; common when inventory may not cover demand.

entries.definition: Funds transferred to settle an obligation—customer paying for a sale or the retailer paying a supplier.
entries.business_usage: Reconciliation, treasury, payment-method reporting, and cash management.

entries.definition: The instrument used to effect payment, such as cash, credit card, debit card, mobile wallet, or gift card.
entries.business_usage: Tender-mix analysis, processing-fee management, and POS configuration.
entries.notes: Inferred; essential for payment processing.

entries.definition: A document from a supplier requesting payment for goods delivered, matched against purchase orders and goods receipts.
entries.business_usage: Three-way match, accounts payable, and cost recognition.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard document in procurement and payables.

entries.definition: A document reducing an amount owed, issued to a customer after a return or to the retailer by a supplier for defective or surplus goods.
entries.business_usage: Returns settlement, accounts adjustments, and net-cost calculations.
entries.notes: Inferred; counterpart to returns and refunds.

entries.definition: The system and moment where a sale is captured in a physical store, generating a transaction record and payment instruction.
entries.business_usage: Checkout operations, tender tracking, and sales auditing.
entries.notes: Inferred from physical-store selling model.

entries.definition: The collection of items purchased by a customer in a single transaction.
entries.business_usage: Basket analysis, cross-sell measurement, and average-transaction-value reporting.
entries.notes: Inferred; core retail analytics concept.

entries.definition: The quantity and value of products held at locations or in transit, maintained to meet customer demand while controlling carrying cost.
entries.business_usage: Stock reporting, replenishment planning, and balance-sheet valuation.

entries.definition: Stock quantities and values attributed to a specific site, enabling store-specific availability and replenishment decisions.
entries.business_usage: Store replenishment, availability displays, and location profitability.

entries.definition: Buffer inventory held above expected demand to absorb variability in supply lead time or sales velocity.
entries.business_usage: Reorder-point formulas, service-level targets, and working-capital trade-offs.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard inventory planning concept.

entries.definition: The inventory level at which a replenishment order is triggered, calculated from demand rate, lead time, and safety stock.
entries.business_usage: Automatic replenishment systems and stock-out prevention.
entries.notes: Inferred; complements safety stock in inventory management.

entries.definition: The percentage of time a product is present and purchasable on the shop floor during trading hours.
entries.business_usage: Service-level tracking, in-store replenishment triggers, and lost-sale estimation.
entries.notes: Inferred; key KPI for physical-store retail.

entries.definition: The number of times inventory is sold and replaced over a period, indicating how efficiently stock is converted to revenue.
entries.business_usage: Buying efficiency reviews, working-capital management, and category benchmarking.
entries.notes: Inferred; core retail inventory KPI.

entries.definition: The estimated number of days current inventory will last at the prevailing rate of sale.
entries.business_usage: Replenishment urgency, overstock detection, and network balancing.
entries.notes: Inferred; complements stock-turn analysis.

entries.definition: The rate at which inventory is sold relative to the quantity received or available, used to judge assortment and pricing effectiveness.
entries.business_usage: Merchandising reviews, clearance decisions, and category performance.
entries.notes: Inferred from selling goods and tracking inventory.

entries.definition: Inventory loss from theft, damage, administrative error, or supplier variance, net of recoveries.
entries.business_usage: Loss prevention, store profitability, and inventory accuracy.
entries.notes: Inferred; common retail concern not mentioned in the brief.

entries.definition: Inventory that has not sold within an expected timeframe and is unlikely to sell at full price without intervention.
entries.business_usage: Markdown planning, write-off decisions, and supplier return negotiations.
entries.notes: Inferred; natural outcome of tracking inventory and sales.

entries.definition: Revenue from sales minus cost of goods sold, expressed as a value or percentage.
entries.business_usage: Category profitability, pricing decisions, and vendor negotiation.
entries.notes: Inferred; primary financial measure for retail trading.

entries.definition: Total sales revenue after deducting returns, refunds, and allowances.
entries.business_usage: Performance reporting, budgeting, and like-for-like comparison.
entries.notes: Inferred; foundational revenue measure for any retailer.

entries.definition: The direct cost attributed to producing or purchasing the goods that were sold during a period.
entries.business_usage: Margin calculation, inventory valuation, and P&L reporting.
entries.notes: Inferred; underpins gross margin and financial monitoring.

entries.definition: The mean monetary value of a sales transaction over a given period.
entries.business_usage: Store performance benchmarking, promotional effectiveness, and upsell measurement.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard retail sales KPI.

entries.definition: The count of customer visits or entries to a physical store within a period.
entries.business_usage: Conversion-rate calculation, marketing attribution, and labour scheduling.
entries.notes: Inferred; important for physical-store performance.

entries.definition: The proportion of store visitors who complete a purchase, indicating sales effectiveness.
entries.business_usage: Store management KPIs, staffing models, and layout optimisation.
entries.notes: Inferred; ties footfall to sales.

entries.definition: Gross Margin Return on Inventory Investment—the ratio of gross margin earned to the average cost of inventory held.
entries.business_usage: Buying efficiency, category investment prioritisation, and range rationalisation.
entries.notes: Inferred; advanced retail performance metric.

entries.definition: The acquisition of goods from suppliers to replenish inventory or fulfil resale demand.
entries.business_usage: Procurement workflows, inbound logistics, and cost-of-goods recognition.

entries.definition: The transfer of goods to a customer in exchange for payment, typically recorded at the line-item level.
entries.business_usage: Revenue recognition, basket analysis, and store performance.

entries.definition: The movement of goods to a destination such as a store, customer address, or pickup point.
entries.business_usage: Logistics planning, promise-date tracking, and delivery performance metrics.

entries.definition: The process of accepting goods back from a customer or returning goods to a supplier, often tied to refunds, exchanges, or credit notes.
entries.business_usage: Reverse logistics, customer policy compliance, and net-sales adjustments.

entries.definition: Revenue-oriented work such as merchandising, pricing, promotions, and customer-facing sales that drive demand and margin.
entries.business_usage: Planning cycles, category management, and commercial KPI reviews.

entries.definition: Day-to-day execution work such as receiving, stocking, fulfilment, and store operations that maintain service levels.
entries.business_usage: Operations scorecards, labour planning, and process improvement.

entries.definition: Monetary flows and accounting events tied to retail operations—receivables, payables, and cash settlement around orders and payments.
entries.business_usage: Cash management, margin reporting, and audit trails.

entries.definition: The ongoing recording and reconciliation of stock quantities and movements across locations and products.
entries.business_usage: Perpetual inventory, cycle counts, and stock accuracy programmes.

entries.definition: The process of restocking a location by triggering purchase orders to suppliers or transfers from other sites when inventory falls below threshold.
entries.business_usage: Automated ordering, shelf-fill rates, and supply-chain planning.
entries.notes: Inferred; connects purchasing, inventory, and availability.

entries.definition: The event of accepting inbound goods against a purchase order or transfer, updating inventory and triggering payables workflows.
entries.business_usage: Receiving operations, inventory accuracy, and three-way match.
entries.notes: Inferred from purchasing and inventory tracking.

entries.definition: Movement of inventory between locations within the retail network to balance supply and demand.
entries.business_usage: Inter-store transfers and network-level stock optimisation.
entries.notes: Inferred from multi-location inventory management.

entries.definition: A periodic physical count of inventory at a location to verify system records and identify discrepancies.
entries.business_usage: Inventory accuracy, shrink detection, and financial audit compliance.
entries.notes: Inferred; essential for stores carrying physical inventory.

entries.definition: A deliberate reduction in selling price to accelerate the sale of slow-moving, seasonal, or excess inventory.
entries.business_usage: Clearance planning, margin-impact analysis, and promotional calendars.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard tool to manage sell-through.

entries.definition: A time-bound commercial offer—such as a discount, multi-buy, or loyalty bonus—designed to stimulate demand for specific products.
entries.business_usage: Promotional calendars, uplift analysis, and supplier-funded deal tracking.
entries.notes: Inferred; core commercial-activity mechanism.

entries.definition: Setting and maintaining the selling price of products based on cost, competition, strategy, and margin targets.
entries.business_usage: Price management systems, competitor benchmarking, and margin control.
entries.notes: Inferred; part of commercial activity.

entries.definition: Repayment to a customer following a return or adjustment, reversing all or part of a prior sale.
entries.business_usage: Cashier procedures, customer service, and net-sales reporting.
entries.notes: Inferred as financial counterpart to returns.

entries.definition: Replacing a returned item with a different product or variant rather than issuing a refund.
entries.business_usage: Customer service policies and returns-reason analysis.
entries.notes: Inferred; alternative outcome of a return.

entries.definition: The process of matching and verifying financial records—such as payments to orders, or invoices to goods receipts—to ensure accuracy.
entries.business_usage: Financial close, exception handling, and audit readiness.
entries.notes: Inferred from monitoring financial activity.

entries.definition: Verification that the purchase order, goods receipt, and supplier invoice agree on quantities and values before approving payment.
entries.business_usage: Accounts payable controls, dispute management, and fraud prevention.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard procurement control.

entries.definition: End-to-end flow from customer order through fulfilment to payment collection and exception handling.
entries.business_usage: Process metrics, DSO-like retail KPIs, and exception queues.
entries.notes: Inferred from monitoring financial activity related to orders and payments.

entries.definition: End-to-end flow from supplier purchase through goods receipt to supplier payment and reconciliation.
entries.business_usage: Procurement efficiency, working-capital management, and supplier relationship reporting.
entries.notes: Inferred from purchasing from suppliers and monitoring payments.

entries.definition: The demand-planning and inventory-positioning cycle that determines how much to buy, when, and where to place it.
entries.business_usage: Demand forecasting, allocation, and pre-season buying.
entries.notes: Inferred; upstream process feeding replenishment.

entries.definition: The final leg of goods movement from a store, warehouse, or hub to the customer's chosen delivery point.
entries.business_usage: Delivery cost modelling, carrier selection, and customer experience.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing deliveries to customers.

entries.definition: A logistics company that transports goods on behalf of the retailer for inbound supply or outbound customer delivery.
entries.business_usage: Freight-cost management, SLA tracking, and delivery-slot scheduling.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing deliveries.

entries.definition: A document accompanying a shipment that lists the contents, quantities, and order reference for the recipient to verify.
entries.business_usage: Goods receipt verification and discrepancy management.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard logistics artifact.

entries.definition: The decomposition of stock levels along product and location dimensions so the business knows what is available where.
entries.business_usage: Store-level availability, allocation rules, and network-wide supply views.

entries.definition: The path through which a sale occurs; in this brief physical stores are primary, with deliveries implying possible ship-to-customer or pickup variants.
entries.business_usage: Channel reporting and inventory segmentation.
entries.notes: Inferred; brief emphasises physical stores.

entries.definition: The amount the retailer pays the supplier per unit, before any rebates, allowances, or landed-cost adjustments.
entries.business_usage: Margin calculation, price setting, and supplier negotiation.
entries.notes: Inferred; necessary for financial monitoring.

entries.definition: The amount charged to the customer for a product at the point of sale, before or after promotional adjustments.
entries.business_usage: POS configuration, margin analysis, and competitive pricing.
entries.notes: Inferred; counterpart to cost price.

entries.definition: The total cost of a product delivered to its destination, including purchase price, freight, duties, and handling.
entries.business_usage: True-cost margin analysis and import-goods management.
entries.notes: Inferred; relevant where delivery logistics affect product cost.

entries.definition: A classification code explaining why a customer or supplier return was initiated, such as defective, wrong item, or change of mind.
entries.business_usage: Root-cause analysis, quality management, and policy refinement.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard data point captured during returns.

entries.definition: A commitment document from the retailer to a supplier specifying products, quantities, prices, and delivery expectations.
entries.business_usage: Order tracking, receiving, and three-way match with invoices.
entries.notes: Inferred; standard procurement artifact.

entries.definition: A unique key for a retail sale or related financial event, tying line items, payments, and returns together.
entries.business_usage: Reconciliation, auditing, and customer lookup.
entries.notes: Inferred from monitoring orders and payments.

entries.definition: A unique short identifier assigned to each store location for use in systems, reporting, and logistics addressing.
entries.business_usage: Location-level filtering, delivery routing, and store-comparison reports.
entries.notes: Inferred from multi-location operations.

entries.definition: A unique identifier assigned to each supplier in the retailer's master data.
entries.business_usage: Purchase-order referencing, payables, and vendor reporting.
entries.notes: Inferred from supplier management.

entries.definition: A unique key assigned to a known customer, often linked to a loyalty card or account registration.
entries.business_usage: Transaction linking, customer service, and CRM.
entries.notes: Inferred from selling to customers.

entries.definition: The current state of an order in its lifecycle—placed, confirmed, picking, shipped, delivered, returned, or cancelled.
entries.business_usage: Customer service, fulfilment dashboards, and SLA tracking.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing deliveries, returns, and financial monitoring of orders.

entries.definition: The settlement state of a payment—pending, authorised, captured, refunded, or failed.
entries.business_usage: Financial reconciliation, exception handling, and cash-position reporting.
entries.notes: Inferred from monitoring financial activity related to payments.

entries.definition: The tracking state of a shipment—dispatched, in transit, out for delivery, delivered, or failed.
entries.business_usage: Customer communication, carrier SLA monitoring, and exception management.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing deliveries.

entries.definition: The processing state of a return—initiated, received, inspected, restocked, or refunded.
entries.business_usage: Reverse-logistics tracking and customer-service SLAs.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing returns.

entries.definition: A qualifier on stock indicating its availability—available, reserved, in transit, damaged, or quarantined.
entries.business_usage: Available-to-promise calculations and warehouse operations.
entries.notes: Inferred from tracking inventory by product and location.

entries.definition: The linkage between an order and one or more payments that settle it, supporting reconciliation and revenue recognition.
entries.business_usage: Cash application, payment matching, and financial close.

entries.definition: The linkage between suppliers and the purchases placed with them, including delivery and payment obligations.
entries.business_usage: Vendor management, payables scheduling, and supply-chain reporting.

entries.definition: The association between a product and its stock positions across locations, enabling availability and replenishment logic.
entries.business_usage: Stock queries, allocation, and availability-to-promise.

entries.definition: The association between a customer and their orders, enabling purchase history, loyalty accrual, and service enquiries.
entries.business_usage: CRM, customer lifetime value, and returns eligibility.
entries.notes: Inferred from selling goods to customers.

entries.definition: The association between an order (or order lines) and the delivery or shipments that fulfil it, enabling tracking and split-shipment handling.
entries.business_usage: Fulfilment monitoring, customer notifications, and SLA compliance.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing deliveries against orders.

entries.definition: The link between a return and its originating order, enabling refund calculation, restocking decisions, and root-cause attribution.
entries.business_usage: Returns processing, customer service, and product-quality feedback loops.
entries.notes: Inferred from managing returns tied to prior sales.

entries.definition: The match between a supplier invoice and its corresponding purchase order and goods receipt, validating amounts before payment.
entries.business_usage: Three-way match, accounts payable, and dispute resolution.
entries.notes: Inferred from procurement and financial monitoring.
